AzuraCast is a free and open-source, self-hosted, all-in-one web radio management suite.

Using AzuraCast and its powerful, intuitive web interface, you can start up a fully working web radio station in a few quick minutes. AzuraCast works for web radio stations of all types and sizes, and is built to run on even the most affordable cloud virtual machines.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with n8n (queue mode)
templates.description: n8n is an open-source workflow automation tool that allows users to connect apps, services, and APIs with a visual, node-based interface. It enables automation of tasks, data processing, and integration between different systems without coding, offering flexibility and customization for various use cases. This template is ideal for automation agencies or power users handling high volumes of workflow executions.

templates.name: Ubuntu 25.04
templates.description: Ubuntu is a computer operating system based on the Debian Linux distribution and distributed as free and open source software, using its own desktop environment. Ubuntu is designed primarily for use on personal computers, although a server edition also exists.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Jellyfin
templates.description: Jellyfin is a free, open-source media server that lets you collect, organize, and stream your personal media like movies, music, and TV shows. It runs on your server and gives you full control to access your content from any device without subscriptions or restrictions.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Chatwoot
templates.description: Chatwoot is an open-source customer engagement platform that enables businesses to manage conversations across multiple communication channels — including website live chat, email, Facebook, WhatsApp, and more — from a single unified inbox. It offers features like real-time messaging, automation, canned responses, team collaboration tools, and analytics, making it ideal for support and sales teams. Chatwoot is designed to be self-hosted or cloud-deployed, giving organizations full control over their data and customization.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Flowise
templates.description: Flowise is an open-source, drag-and-drop tool for visually building and deploying LLM (Large Language Model) applications. It simplifies the creation of AI workflows by connecting components like prompts, models, and APIs without requiring extensive coding knowledge.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Windmill
templates.description: Windmill is an open-source developer platform and workflow engine that transforms scripts into production-ready applications, APIs, and automated workflows. It supports multiple programming languages—including Python, TypeScript, Go.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Airflow
templates.description: Apache Airflow is an open-source platform for authoring, scheduling, and monitoring workflows as directed acyclic graphs (DAGs). Designed for scalability and flexibility, it allows users to programmatically define complex data pipelines and automate tasks across a wide range of systems.

templates.name: Fedora Cloud 42
templates.description: Fedora Cloud is a streamlined version of the Fedora operating system tailored for cloud computing, offering a minimal footprint for containerized applications. It supports cutting-edge cloud technologies like Docker and Kubernetes, facilitating efficient container management. Ideal for developers and sysadmins, Fedora Cloud ensures fast, secure, and scalable deployments in both public and private cloud environments.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Home Assistant
templates.description: Home Assistant is an open-source platform that allows you to control, automate, and monitor smart home devices from a single, privacy-focused dashboard. It supports thousands of integrations and runs entirely locally, giving you full control over your home automation setup.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Node-RED
templates.description: Node-RED is a low-code programming tool for wiring together hardware devices, APIs, and online services using a visual flow-based interface. Built on Node.js, it enables users to create automations and IoT applications quickly by connecting blocks of logic without extensive coding.

templates.name: AlmaLinux 10
templates.description: AlmaLinux is a free and open source Linux distribution, created originally by CloudLinux to provide a community-supported, production-grade enterprise operating system that is binary-compatible with Red Hat Enterprise Linux.

templates.name: Rocky Linux 10
templates.description: Rocky Linux is a Linux distribution developed by Rocky Enterprise Software Foundation. It is intended to be a downstream, complete binary-compatible release using the Red Hat Enterprise Linux operating system source code.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with HestiaCP
templates.description: HestiaCP - Simple and clear interface. Instead of adding more elements to work with, this control panel prefer to remove as much as possible. The main goal of this panel was to improve the ergonomics by reducing unnecessary movements and operations. It is all about using less, because less is more.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with n8n (+100 workflows)
templates.description: n8n is an open-source workflow automation tool that allows users to connect apps, services, and APIs with a visual, node-based interface. It enables automation of tasks, data processing, and integration between different systems without coding, offering flexibility and customization for various use cases. This template offers you 100+ most popular community workflows pre-installed into your n8n instance.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with MCP Server
templates.description: Launches a Remote MCP Server with Python, FastMCP, Docker, and an SEO analyzer example. Works with Cursor, Windsurf, and other MCP clients. Just write Python functions to build MCP tools like SSL checkers, data pipelines, and web health monitors - no frontend needed. Perfect for AI engineers, technical SEOs, devs, and tool builders.

templates.name: Debian 13
templates.description: Debian is a computer operating system composed of software packages released as free and open source software primarily under the GNU General Public License along with other free software licenses. Debian GNU/Linux, which includes the GNU OS tools and Linux kernel, is a popular and influential Linux distribution. It is distributed with access to repositories containing thousands of software packages ready for installation and use. Debian is known for relatively strict adherence to the philosophies of Unix and free software as well as using collaborative software development and testing processes.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Claude Code
templates.description: Claude Code is a command-line interface (CLI) for interacting with Anthropic’s Claude language model directly from your terminal. It enables users to run prompts, manage conversations, and automate workflows with Claude using simple commands. Ideal for developers and power users, Claude Code makes it easy to integrate Claude into scripts and development environments without relying on a graphical interface.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with WireGuard
templates.description: wg-easy is a web-based user interface for managing WireGuard VPN servers easily and visually. It simplifies the setup and administration of WireGuard by providing a clean dashboard where you can add or remove clients, view QR codes for quick configuration, and monitor connection status—all without editing config files manually.

templates.name: Debian 13 with Game Panel
templates.description: Game Panel is a Minecraft, Counter-Strike and other Steam games server control panel that allows to manage multiple servers from a single web based user interface.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Posthog
templates.description: PostHog is an open-source product analytics platform that helps teams understand user behavior and improve their software. It offers features like event tracking, session recording, and feature flags, all while allowing data to be stored privately on your own infrastructure. With PostHog, companies can gain deep insights without relying on third-party analytics services.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Sim Studio
templates.description: Sim is an open-source, visual workflow builder for AI agents. It lets you design, test, and deploy LLM-powered workflows through a simple drag-and-drop interface, connecting with tools like Slack, Gmail, Notion and databases. With support for multiple models, structured outputs, and flexible deployment options, Sim makes building and managing AI automations intuitive and collaborative - all without heavy coding

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Immich
templates.description: Immich is an open-source, self-hosted photo and video backup solution designed for mobile devices. It lets you securely store, manage, and access your media on your own server, offering features similar to Google Photos but with full control over your data.

templates.name: Rocky Linux 9 with IceWarp
templates.description: IceWarp is a unified communications and collaboration platform that combines email, team chat, voice and video calls, document editing, and file sharing into one solution. It’s designed for businesses that want a secure, all-in-one alternative to services like Microsoft 365 or Google Workspace.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Mattermost
templates.description: Mattermost is an open-source team collaboration platform offering secure messaging, file sharing, and integrations. Self-hosted alternative to Slack for organizations needing data control.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Odoo
templates.description: Odoo is an open-source, integrated business management software with a modular architecture, supporting functions like CRM, e-commerce, accounting, and project management. Designed for scalability and efficiency, it allows businesses to customize and expand their toolset as needed. Odoo's user-friendly interface and strong community support make it a versatile choice for businesses aiming to streamline operations and enhance productivity.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Supabase
templates.description: Supabase is an open-source backend-as-a-service (BaaS) platform that provides tools for building web and mobile applications. It offers features like a real-time database, authentication, storage, and APIs, making it easier for developers to create and manage apps without complex server setup. Built on top of PostgreSQL, Supabase emphasizes simplicity and scalability with a developer-friendly interface.

templates.name: Ubuntu 25.10
templates.description: Ubuntu is a computer operating system based on the Debian Linux distribution and distributed as free and open source software, using its own desktop environment. Ubuntu is designed primarily for use on personal computers, although a server edition also exists.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Gemini CLI
templates.description: Gemini CLI is a command-line interface for interacting with Google’s Gemini AI models directly from your server. It allows developers to run prompts, generate content, test model capabilities, and integrate AI into scripts and workflows—all through a simple and powerful terminal-based tool.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with MEAN Stack (MongoDB, ExpressJS, AngularJS, NodeJS)
templates.description: The MEAN stack is a full-stack development framework that leverages four key technologies: MongoDB (a NoSQL database), Express.js (a web application framework), Angular (a front-end framework), and Node.js (a JavaScript runtime environment). Together, these components enable developers to build scalable and dynamic web applications entirely using JavaScript across the entire development stack.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with MERN Stack (MongoDB, ExpressJS, ReactJS, NodeJS)
templates.description: The MERN stack is a web development stack that incorporates MongoDB, Express.js, React, and Node.js, providing a unified JavaScript-based framework for building scalable and efficient applications.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with MEVN Stack (MongoDB, ExpressJS, VueJS, NodeJS)
templates.description: The MEVN stack is a modern web development stack consisting of MongoDB, Express.js, Vue.js, and Node.js. It allows developers to build full-stack web applications using JavaScript across the entire development stack.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Docker and Traefik
templates.description: Docker is a modern containerization platform that streamlines how applications are developed, shipped, and deployed. In this template, Docker is paired with Traefik, a powerful reverse proxy and load balancer, to provide automatic routing, SSL management, and secure access to your containers—ensuring consistent, scalable, and production-ready deployments across environments.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with Cosmos
templates.description: Cosmos is an open-source self-hosted platform for managing applications, containers, and servers through a unified web interface. It simplifies deploying and running Docker-based services by providing built-in tools for networking, security, reverse proxy, and application management.

templates.name: Ubuntu 24.04 with NemoClaw
templates.description: Ubuntu 24.04 with NemoClaw is a ready-to-use VPS template that comes with NVIDIA NemoClaw preinstalled, enabling you to deploy and manage large language model workflows with ease.
